_description: The Chapter 1 momentum registry — the flat list of all live trajectories the MomentumAdvancer ticks every simulation tick. This file is what the World Compiler would produce from wang_family_village.blueprint.json (see worldgen/blueprint/v2/compiled_world_state/momentum_registry.schema.json). The simulation NEVER queries the blueprint at runtime. It ticks THIS file. Each entry carries its schema_ref so the advancer knows how to advance it (GDC-L1-SYS-0004: feedback loop polarity). Per Art XXXIX, these trajectories were in motion before the player existed and would continue if the player never existed. Per Art XL, every trajectory listed here must produce at least one SPEC status Living Moment (see living_moments_ledger.json) within three cycles — or it is architecture before experience.
_article: XXXIX (Reality Has Momentum) + XL (Prove The Experience Before The Architecture)
_gdc_ref: GDC-L1-SYS-0004 (feedback loop polarity — each trajectory is a named feedback loop: positive/reinforcing or negative/balancing, with drivers, thresholds, and interruptibility)
tick_zero_note: The simulation_seed determines initial variance: which herb is ripest, which rumor has spread furthest, whether the patriarch is closer or farther from breakthrough, exactly where the wolf pack's territory edge sits. The STAGE is fixed (canon_seed). The ACTORS are mid-motion at varying points (simulation_seed).

trajectories.loop_description: Extraction exceeds recharge. The vein is slowly draining. A formation could restore it; none exists. This is a slowly-spiraling negative loop — the weaker the vein, the fewer herbs, the less to trade, the more the village declines, the more cultivators leave, the more extraction concentrates on fewer people... until the village cannot sustain cultivation at all.
drivers: village cultivation draws 0.00030/tick
no recharge formation in place
seasonal oscillation +/-0.04 over 24000 ticks

thresholds.consequence: cultivation becomes impractical; village enters terminal decline
a cultivator could harvest at elevated qi

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Shrine spirit lamp flickers weakly. Cultivators meditate longer for less gain. Common spirit herbs are scarce within a day's foraging. Old villagers remark the qi used to be thicker.

trajectories.loop_description: Prey density is falling. The pack expands territory to compensate. Expansion depletes prey further in the new zone. The pack grows bolder, pushes closer to the village. Livestock pressure rises. This is a positive reinforcing loop that, unchecked, ends in a raid or a cull — the canonical Memory Metric driver (Art XXXI.5).
drivers: prey_density 0.28, declining — over-hunted the north forest
autumn trending winter — seasonal pressure rising
livestock_pressure 0.34 — two goats taken this season (memory_ledger event at tick -6000)
distance 240 blocks and closing (was 600 last season)

thresholds.consequence: Old Chen's dog dies. The canonical Memory Metric event (Art XXXI.5). NOT scripted — emerges from this trajectory crossing its threshold.
village enters fear trajectory; patrols may be organized

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Howls closer than last moon. Old Chen's gate has no dog. Goat skeleton in the south pasture. Tracks at the village edge in snow. A child tells a story about a goat that died — with the details wrong (seven wolves, not five).

trajectories.loop_description: The south bank was deforested two generations ago. Erosion concentrated there. The bridge abutment is cracking. When it fails, trade drops, the village's decline steepens, and the village has even fewer resources to repair it — a negative spiral. This is the canonical history-vs-momentum example (Art XXXIX.1): the bridge collapse is history; the village dying because trade never recovered is momentum.
drivers: south bank deforested two generations ago — erosion concentrated
autumn rains raising flow_force — flood risk high (1.6x seasonal multiplier)
no repair action taken — the village cannot agree on the cost

thresholds.consequence: bridge collapses. Caravans detour. Trade visits drop. Village decline steepens. IRREVERSIBLE without rebuild.
cultivator can forage spirit minerals from riverbed

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Bridge south abutment cracked through, moss in the fissures. The river gives underfoot when crossing. Old marker post stands in water now. At the west bend, something glitters in the shallows. Innkeeper says the bridge has needed repair for two years.

trajectories.loop_description: The recruiter is already en route when the player arrives. This is not a loop — it is a one-shot transit with a terminus (arrival at the village). But it feeds multiple other loops: the village's hope/fear for their children, the rumor's confirmation/disproof, the economy's supply event. The transit itself is perceivable (tracks, merchant's mention) before the recruiter arrives.
drivers: Heng Yue recruitment_status = active (the sect needs disciples)
route: seg_crossroads_to_pass, 1400 blocks, 9000 ticks ETA
supplies adequate, morale good, threat low

thresholds.consequence: recruiter arrives; tests children for spirit roots; may recruit one (canon event). Economy supply event. Rumor confirmed. Village hope/fear peaks.

thresholds.risk: low on current segment; rises on seg_wang_to_crossroads if bandit_group_south enters raid_blitz

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Fresh footprints on the road from Zhao crossroads, heading toward the village. A merchant mentions a young man in Heng Yue robes heading northeast. The village head says 'perhaps within the month' — but the tracks suggest sooner.

trajectories.loop_description: The crimson root patch is growing toward peak maturity. Wang Lin has already half-harvested it. The patch sits over a node of the spirit vein — its growth rate depends on qi_density. This is the resource tension: Wang Lin needs the herbs for his restriction study, the patch is days from peak, and the vein is thinning (which will slow future growth). A cultivator who finds it faces a choice: take now (weak), wait (risk losing it), guard it (reveal presence).
drivers: spirit_vein_wang_village qi_density 0.41 — adequate (patch is over a vein node)
autumn — growth moderate
last harvested 6000 ticks ago by npc_wang_lin (took 4 of 16 — the half-harvested cue)

thresholds.consequence: max potency; if unharvested, decay countdown begins

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: A cluster of crimson root in the west wood, half-picked — four stalks cut cleanly, eight still growing, the cut marks less than three days old. Someone was here and will return. The roots are budding but not yet flowered — a cultivator knows they are days from peak.

trajectories.loop_description: Supplies are falling. Desperation is rising. When desperation crosses 0.7, the bandits enter raid_blitz — multiple rapid attacks on the southern road. This endangers caravans (including the recruiter), raises trade costs, and may trigger a patrol response from Heng Yue. It is a positive reinforcing loop: raids deplete supplies of the raided, raising regional scarcity, making more targets desperate, creating more bandits. The current trajectory is approaching the blitz threshold.
drivers: supplies 0.31 and falling — consuming faster than raids replenish
desperation 0.68, approaching raid_blitz threshold (0.7)
south hills picked clean — no forage income
last raid 4000 ticks ago — a caravan on the southern road; perceivable as a burnt wagon

thresholds.consequence: southern road becomes actively dangerous; multiple caravans hit; recruiter at risk

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: A burnt wagon on the southern road, the cargo scattered and picked over, less than a week old. The innkeeper warns travelers not to go south after dark. Smoke rises from the south hills in the evening — a campfire, but whose? A merchant at the crossroads mentions two caravans were hit this season where there used to be none.

trajectories.loop_description: The rumor that Heng Yue is sending a recruiter is two weeks old. It has spread to two neighboring settlements and already forked (east hamlet thinks next month, zhao_market thinks testing for a specific spirit root). Belief is slowly decaying. If the recruiter arrives within the decay window, the rumor is confirmed and transforms into a remembered fact (memory_ledger entry). If not, it fades. The rumor demonstrates that information in this world has its own momentum — it spreads, distorts, decays, and is remembered or forgotten independently of the player.
drivers: origin: a villager saw recruiter robes at the crossroads two weeks ago
spread: peddler to zhao_market, farmer to east_hamlet
distortion: east hamlet thinks next month; zhao market thinks testing for specific root

thresholds.consequence: recruiter arrives; belief spikes; rumor transforms to memory_ledger fact

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Two villagers at the well disagree about whether the recruiter comes this month or next. A merchant from the east hamlet adds that the sect is looking for a specific spirit root (it isn't — that's distortion). A child says 'my cousin says maybe, but grandmother says not this year.' The rumor has age, detail, and disagreement.

trajectories.schema_ref: derived from settlement_graph viability queries (implicit momentum)
trajectories.loop_description: The village is slowly dying. Not catastrophically — quietly. Two families have left in the last generation. The spirit-rooted young are taken by Heng Yue and do not return. The vein is thinning. The bridge is failing. The wolves are closing. Trade is dropping. Each driver reinforces the others: fewer people means less capacity to repair the bridge, which means less trade, which means fewer resources, which means more people leave. This is the master negative loop of Chapter 1 — the stage the player arrives into.

rate_of_change.description: slow, compounding — each sub-trajectory's threshold crossing steepens the decline

drivers: spirit_vein qi_density declining (T001)
bridge integrity declining (T003)
wolf territory closing (T002)
Heng Yue recruiter takes spirit-rooted young (T005)

thresholds.description: when enough sub-trajectories cross their thresholds simultaneously, the village enters a death spiral from which it cannot recover without external intervention

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Abandoned house-sites at the village edge, roofs caved in. Shrine incense is thin. The elder pauses before answering how the village fares. A child mentions her friend's family left last winter.

trajectories.loop_description: Wang Lin is stuck on the third layer of the cave restriction. His crimson root supplements are running low (the west patch is his source, 8000 ticks from peak). His progress is slow because the vein is thin. If he exhausts his supplements before the patch matures, his progress stalls — a timing tension. The player who demonstrates restriction skill becomes relevant (Art XXXI.2: Wang Lin evaluates understanding/harm/motive/judgment/timing, then may accept aid). This is the bidirectional-Wang-Lin opportunity.
drivers: stuck on the third layer — cannot decipher alone
crimson root supplements at 0.30 — depleting
qi from spirit_vein_wang_village is thin — slowing accumulation

thresholds.consequence: progress stalls; Wang Lin must harvest more crimson root (west patch 8000 ticks from peak — timing tension)
Wang Lin deciphers layer 3; capability_score rises; new opportunity for player

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: Wang Lin is often absent from the village — at the cave above, studying. A villager mentions it offhand. The cave entrance shows worn path, old incense stubs. Wang Lin, when present, is distracted and irritable. His hands smell of crimson root. None of this is a quest.

trajectories.loop_description: The village's grudge against the Teng-ally merchant is festering. It started over a trade dispute twelve thousand ticks ago. The grudge affects trade terms (the merchant overcharges), which worsens the village's economy, which deepens the grievance. If it reaches flashpoint, it produces an open confrontation — the elder may ban the merchant, cutting off a trade lifeline, or the merchant may retaliate. The grudge is a slow-burn positive loop.
drivers: trade dispute origin — overcharging on rice after the bridge damage raised costs
the merchant's ally (Teng family) is the village's overlord — power asymmetry suppresses open conflict
no resolution mechanism — the elder is too weak to challenge the Teng family directly

thresholds.consequence: open confrontation; elder may ban the merchant; Teng family may retaliate

thresholds.conditions: if trade normalizes (bridge repaired, new merchant) the grudge could decay — but the bridge is failing, not being repaired

trajectories.perceivable_without_notification: The Teng-ally merchant's prices are visibly higher than a traveler would expect. The elder goes silent when the merchant is mentioned. Trade at the market stall is cold and brief — no pleasantries. A village woman mutters that the rice was better last year when the other merchant still came.

cascade_map._description: Which trajectories feed into which others. When T001 (spirit vein) crosses a threshold, it cascades to T009 (village decline) and T006 (herb maturation) and T010 (Wang Lin's study). This map tells the MomentumAdvancer which trajectories to re-evaluate when a threshold fires.

tick_protocol._description: The five operations the MomentumAdvancer performs each tick, per momentum_registry.schema.json.
tick_protocol.1_advance: For each trajectory, apply rate_of_change to current_state for one tick.
tick_protocol.2_check_thresholds: For each trajectory, compare current_state against threshold values. If a threshold is crossed, fire the consequence and log to memory_ledger.
tick_protocol.3_propagate_cascades: If a threshold fired, re-evaluate all trajectories in cascade_map[fired_trajectory].
tick_protocol.4_update_perceivability: Recompute perceivable_without_notification based on new current_state. Update spatial_index if entity positions changed.
tick_protocol.5_log: Append tick delta to trajectory history (for debugging and for the Memory Metric — the world remembers because trajectories leave traces).
